name: GT-bh-cstar
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:458-466 (1.3.2), VERBATIM: "$\mathscr{A}$  is called a Banach algebra if  $\|xy\| \le \|x\| \|y\|$ ..." and "If H is a complex Hilbert space then B(H), the bounded linear operators on H, is a  $C^*$  algebra in the usual operator norm" and "x is called positive, written  $x \ge 0$ , if x is of the form  $x = y^*y$ ". Hence in B(H): for a=a^* self-adjoint, a^2=a^*a is positive, a^2>=0; and the operator norm is the order-unit norm on B(H)_sa.
